buy_side_bullets: Capital wars risk emerging — tariff escalation 1-2% GDP drag could force stagflationary Fed cuts even as inflation runs 4-5%. [Dalio]
US equities most expensive in fund's history — capital preservation over returns; fund closed to new investors July 1. [Einhorn]
$700B hyperscaler AI capex is durable compounding — META and AMZN called 'cheap'; contrarian entry after worst hyperscaler month in years. [Ackman shift]
Micron short at historically extreme 200-day MA extension (most stretched since 1984) — AI memory bubble forming alongside NVDA/PLTR puts. [Burry evolving]
Tactical shift to shorter-dated government bonds — Warsh's hawkish credibility allows Fed to hold without hiking. [Gundlach shift]
sell_side_bullets: Largest speculative bubble since 19th-century railroad boom — 25/25/25/25 stock/bond/cash/commodity portfolio the defensive response. [Hartnett evolving]
Hyperscaler free cash flow already rolling over; US equities in earnings bubble — 'Wiley E. Coyote' moment approaching in H2. [Berezin]
End of Great Moderation entering Temperamental Era (1960s-1990s analog) — IT driving 55% of earnings growth at 31% of index weight. [Sonders]
S&P 500 target raised to 8,250 after gangbusters Q1 earnings; BRAIN Revolution extends Roaring 2020s. [Yardeni]
'Sturdy growth, stagnant jobs, stable prices' — US GDP +2.6% above consensus; Goldman dropped recession concern entirely. [Hatzius]

Tepper's 40% cash position signals deep skepticism about market breadth despite Appaloosa's 32% H1 performance

Berezin's earnings bubble thesis directly contradicts market consensus of continued strong AI-driven earnings growth

aligned: Fed unlikely to cut meaningfully in 2026 — Gundlach (hold), Yardeni (one cut only), Hartnett all agree on very limited Fed easing cycle

Fed paralysis: elevated yields acting as passive tightening; Warsh credibility enables hold without hiking; cuts structurally unjustifiable

buy_side_bullets: US equities most expensive in fund's history — capital preservation over returns; closed to new investors July 1 at peak valuations. [Einhorn]

$700B hyperscaler AI capex is durable compounding — META and AMZN cheap at scale; contrarian entry after worst hyperscaler month in years. [Ackman shift]

2027 recession risk 'very significant' as AI capex peaks and fiscal stimulus faces gridlock — AI spending pulling forward demand that creates a 2027 vacuum. [Rosenberg]

Hyperscaler FCF rolling over; US equities in earnings bubble — 'Wiley E. Coyote' moment approaching; H2 2026 Great Rotation then broad selloff. [Berezin]
Double-digit inflation heading toward 1970s levels — surging bank lending adding new transmission channel beyond fiscal dominance; Ice Age thesis officially dead. [Edwards]

Overheating now the dominant risk replacing stagflation — three structural drivers (AI, industrial renaissance, Big Beautiful Bill) adding +2.2% GDP; NAIRU binding for 57 months. [Slok shift]

Double-digit inflation pathway — bank lending added new transmission channel; fiscal dominance + credit expansion + geopolitical shocks = 1970s replay. [Edwards]
